channel.title: State-Policy Think-Tank Digest
channel.description: Latest posts from free-market policy institutes across the U.S.

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] RGF on with Bob Clark for TWO fantastic interviews
item.description:
On Thursday, June 25, 2026 RGF hosted a luncheon with speaker Tim Sandefur as part of the organization’s “America 250” celebrations. Tim signed copies of his book: “Proclaiming Liberty: John Adams, Thomas Jefferson, and the Declaration of Independence.” You can check out the interview Bob Clark of KKOB radio did with Tim below: The following day Paul […]

The post RGF on with Bob Clark for TWO fantastic interviews appeared first on Rio Grande Foundation.

item.title: [Empowerms] Andy Taggart: The Ideas That Made America
item.description:
Andy Taggart: The Ideas That Made America As America approaches its 250th anniversary, what exactly are we celebrating? In this episode, Grant sits down with attorney, writer, and student of history Andy Taggart to explore the ideas behind the Declaration of Independence and why they remain relevant nearly two and a half centuries later. They…

The post Andy Taggart: The Ideas That Made America first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Nebraska’s Child Care Costs Are Holding Back Young Families
item.description:
We recently posted about childcare regulations, and Nebraska’s solid standing according to the Archbridge Institute. But there are lots of data points to consider when it comes to childcare issues, and how childcare influences a state’s workforce, and another one showed up today, from Wallethub–childcare costs. When we talk about Nebraska’s outmigration problem, we often...

The post Nebraska’s Child Care Costs Are Holding Back Young Families appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Newjerseypolicyinstitute] NJPI: Largest Budget in State History Falls Short on Transparency and Fiscal Responsibility
item.description:
FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE   Parsippany, NJ – The New Jersey Policy Institute today expressed disappointment with the process surrounding the adoption […]

The post NJPI: Largest Budget in State History Falls Short on Transparency and Fiscal Responsibility first appeared on New Jersey Policy Institute.

item.title: [Cardinalinstitute] Special Circumstance Reviews
item.description:
Special Circumstance Reviews Between 2023 and 2025, the West Virginia Board of Education launched nine Special Circumstance Reviews — structured state investigations into county school systems exhibiting signs of serious failure. These reviews, grounded in WV Code §18-2E-5 and WVBE Policy 2322, culminated in state takeovers of multiple districts. Taken together, they reveal not a […]

The post Special Circumstance Reviews appeared first on Cardinal Institute.

item.title: [Cardinalinstitute] Regulatory Reform in West Virginia
item.description:
Regulatory Reform in West Virginia The Problem: A State Buried in Red Tape West Virginia has accumulated one of the heaviest regulatory burdens in the nation. According to the State RegData project, the state now has 131,007 regulatory restrictions on the books — mandatory rules containing language like “shall,” “must,” and “may not” that impose […]

The post Regulatory Reform in West Virginia appeared first on Cardinal Institute.

item.title: [Cardinalinstitute] Charity Care
item.description:
Charity Care West Virginia non-profit hospitals enjoy billions in tax exemptions. In return, the social contract demands they provide meaningful charity care to struggling families. The data shows they are not holding up their end of the bargain. This brief examines the evidence — from IRS filings, Medicare cost reports, and federal subsidy data — […]

The post Charity Care appeared first on Cardinal Institute.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] Record Month
item.description:
Every Fourth of July, Americans celebrate the ideals that founded this country — national sovereignty, individual liberty, self-determination and the right to make our own choices. At the Freedom Foundation, we see those principles in action every day. June was a record-breaking month for the organization, as 6,827 public employees exercised their rights and opted […]

The post Record Month appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Cascadepolicy] The Last Living Signer’s “Best Earthly Inheritance”
item.description:
On the 50th anniversary of America’s Independence, the Declaration’s last living signer, Charles Carroll, left a final message every generation still needs to hear. Two hundred fifty years ago this July 4, the world received a gift as one of the most consequential political documents ever written. When Thomas Jefferson drafted the Declaration of Independence, he boldly declared […]

The post The Last Living Signer’s “Best Earthly Inheritance” appeared first on Cascade Policy Institute.

item.title: [Jbartlett] Why Josiah Bartlett held New Hampshire’s vote for independence to the last moment
item.description:
In 1776, Josiah Bartlett really could’ve used a good cell phone plan. Stuck in sweltering Philadelphia as July crawled ever closer on the calendar, the Josiah Bartlett Center for Public Policy’s namesake Founding Father was getting nervous.  New Hampshire’s lead delegate to the Continental Congress, Bartlett was a passionate patriot. His first year as a […]

The post Why Josiah Bartlett held New Hampshire’s vote for independence to the last moment appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Opportunityarkansas] Record State Surplus Signals Need for Income Tax Relief
item.description:
Arkansas has now collected multiple record-breaking surpluses in recent years. It’s time to return this money to taxpayers by cutting the state income tax and giving working Arkansans a pay raise.

The post Record State Surplus Signals Need for Income Tax Relief first appeared on Opportunity Arkansas.

item.title: [Empowerms] Stewarding the American Dream
item.description:
As America approaches its 250th anniversary, I have found myself thinking less about politics and more about stewardship. Recently, Secretary of State Marco Rubio described America as “a story of perpetual improvement,” a nation where each generation leaves the next freer, safer, and more prosperous than the one before. That’s the essence of stewardship. The…

The post Stewarding the American Dream first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Opportunityarkansas] Arkansas Voters Once Again Reject Attack on Education Freedom, Families, and Taxpayers
item.description:
Radical education amendment again fails to qualify for ballot

The post Arkansas Voters Once Again Reject Attack on Education Freedom, Families, and Taxpayers first appeared on Opportunity Arkansas.

item.title: [Beacontn] What’s the tea on the TEA—and teachers’ unions as a whole?
item.description: If you’re a teacher, do yourself a favor: keep your hard-earned dollars. Unions like the TEA aren’t brewed for you.

item.title: [Beacontn] The Pros of AI and the Removal of CONs
item.description: In the same way that MRIs and CT scans were cutting-edge innovations that changed the game on early diagnosis and treatment, AI should be welcomed, not stifled by government red tape.

item.title: [Platteinstitute] How Lincoln Raised Property Taxes Without Raising Rates
item.description:
Lincoln’s new budget levies the same property tax rate as last year, with Mayor Gaylor Baird boasting that “except for voter-approved stormwater bonds, the city of Lincoln has not increased the property tax rate during my administration.” The statement is accurate—and yet Lincoln homeowners’ property tax bills will increase under the new budget. Same rate,...

The post How Lincoln Raised Property Taxes Without Raising Rates appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Empirecenter] The Attorney General’s MFCU SNAFU
item.description:
Attorney General Letitia James’ latest fight with the Trump administration focuses on New York’s Medicaid Fraud Control Unit, a federally....

The post The Attorney General’s MFCU SNAFU appeared first on Empire Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Newjerseypolicyinstitute] Affordability Requires More Energy, Not More Targets
item.description:
Governor Mikie Sherrill signed a package of energy affordability bills this week and announced $90 million in immediate relief for […]

The post Affordability Requires More Energy, Not More Targets first appeared on New Jersey Policy Institute.

item.title: [Mainepolicy] Maine’s Bell-to-Bell Cell Phone Ban Goes Too Far
item.description:
By 2026 Maine Policy Institute Public Policy Intern Andrew Cook Maine’s new statewide ban on student use of cellphones in school is based on reasonable concerns: Cell phones and other electronic devices can be distracting in class, and teachers should not have to compete with group chats, games, or social media while trying to teach. […]

The post Maine’s Bell-to-Bell Cell Phone Ban Goes Too Far appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Will Law] WILL Sues Gov. Evers over $250 Million Discriminatory Program
item.description:
A Wisconsin-based taxpayer association, represented by WILL, filed a new lawsuit challenging the Wisconsin Supplier Diversity Program, a procurement program that spends over $250 million each year purchasing supplies and services based on race. 

The post WILL Sues Gov. Evers over $250 Million Discriminatory Program appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Jamesmadison] Florida Politics: Data Center Protests Are Hurting Main Street
item.description:
Data center opposition has become a national trend, uniting Americans across the country and across the political spectrum....

The post Florida Politics: Data Center Protests Are Hurting Main Street appeared first on James Madison Institute.

item.title: [Will Law] Citizens Defending Liberty v. Evers
item.description:
WILL filed a new lawsuit challenging the Wisconsin Supplier Diversity Program, a procurement program that spends over $250 million each year purchasing supplies and services based on race. White, Middle Eastern, and most Asian small-business owners are ineligible for this program simply because of their race.

The post Citizens Defending Liberty v. Evers appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Cascadepolicy] The 21st Century Road to No Affordable Housing
item.description:
by Randal O’Toole Why Congress’s Housing Act is Just Political Theater A recent bill passed by Congress aimed at making housing more affordable will lead to almost no improvements in affordability. In fact, some provisions of the law will make housing even less affordable than it is today. Imagine that, in response to an airline […]

The post The 21st Century Road to No Affordable Housing appeared first on Cascade Policy Institute.

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] How to strengthen the climb from the safety net
item.description:
Wisconsin’s next governor and Legislature should strengthen the state’s safety net by focusing it more clearly on work, family stability and upward mobility.

The post How to strengthen the climb from the safety net appeared first on Badger Institute.

item.title: [Jamesmadison] General Election Statewide TV Debates Announced in Florida’s 2026 Races for U.S. Senator, Governor
item.description:
General Election Statewide TV Debates Announced in Florida’s 2026 Races for U.S. Senator, Governor The respected, effective non-partisan partnership...

The post General Election Statewide TV Debates Announced in Florida’s 2026 Races for U.S. Senator, Governor appeared first on James Madison Institute.

item.title: [Jbartlett] In New England, low-tax New Hampshire has smallest percentage of residents who want to leave
item.description:
Granite Staters are told often that our low taxes are a liability and that copying the other New England state’s higher-tax, higher-spending model would improve affordability and the overall quality of life. New polling from the University of New Hampshire Survey Center suggests that the exact opposite is true. A series of UNH surveys released […]

The post In New England, low-tax New Hampshire has smallest percentage of residents who want to leave appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] Ninth Circuit Hears CPC’s First Amendment Challenge to SB 399
item.description:
On Tuesday, the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Ninth Circuit heard oral arguments in California Policy Center v. Garcia-Brower, CPC’s lawsuit challenging California Senate Bill 399, a law that violates employers’ First Amendment rights by prohibiting the discussion of “religious or political matters” during mandatory workplace meetings. Jeffrey Schwab, Senior Counsel with Liberty Justice Center, presented...

The post Ninth Circuit Hears CPC’s First Amendment Challenge to SB 399 first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Case Study: Papillion and Missing Middle Housing
item.description:
This is the first in a series of “Research Notes” compiled by our summer intern, Ethan Roth. We have him looking at an assortment of things related to housing in Nebraska. We thought others might be interested in some of the things that he is seeing. These findings will add to our base of knowledge...

The post Case Study: Papillion and Missing Middle Housing appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] RGF Opinion piece: Rebates now, tax reform later
item.description:
The following appeared in Las Cruces Sun News on July 11, 2027 Gov. Michelle Lujan Grisham is to be applauded for calling for rebates for hard-pressed New Mexicans. With an additional $850 million available to the State due to elevated oil and gas prices because of the Iran War, New Mexicans should receive some help […]

The post RGF Opinion piece: Rebates now, tax reform later appeared first on Rio Grande Foundation.

item.title: [Will Law] WI Appeals Court Rules in Favor of Property Owners in Vacation Rental Dispute
item.description:
WILL secured a major legal victory for property owners in Sister Bay after the Wisconsin Court of Appeals ruled that the Village's four-bedroom cap for vacation rentals operates as an illegal "back-door prohibition on renting."

The post WI Appeals Court Rules in Favor of Property Owners in Vacation Rental Dispute appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Idahofreedom] The Ratchet Only Goes One Way: Boise’s Ever Increasing Property Taxes
item.description: A brief history of Boise property taxes reveals only one trend: they are increasing. Property taxes have increased every fiscal year since FY21. The only reason FY21 was spared was due to pandemic-era funding from state and federal sources. In FY22, the base increase was 3%, in FY23 it was 2.45%, and in FY24 it […]

item.title: [Alabamapolicy] Alabama Policy Institute Launches Landmark Alabama Tax Relief Report: A Multi-Phase Roadmap for Economic Growth and Fiscal Discipline
item.description:
Alabama Policy Institute Launches Landmark Alabama Tax Relief Report: A Multi-Phase Roadmap for Economic Growth and Fiscal Discipline BIRMINGHAM, AL — The Alabama Policy Institute (API) today announced the official launch of its highly anticipated publication, the Alabama Tax Relief Report: Next Steps. The comprehensive research report arrives at a critical juncture for the state’s fiscal future, […]

The post Alabama Policy Institute Launches Landmark Alabama Tax Relief Report: A Multi-Phase Roadmap for Economic Growth and Fiscal Discipline appeared first on Alabama Policy Institute.

item.title: [Newjerseypolicyinstitute] New Jersey Launches Process for New Nuclear Generation
item.description:
Governor Mikie Sherrill has signed the Power New Jersey Act, marking a significant shift in the state’s long-term energy strategy […]

The post New Jersey Launches Process for New Nuclear Generation first appeared on New Jersey Policy Institute.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] Commercializing Hydrocarbon Production from Air and Water
item.description:
Whenever evaluating a new energy technology, caution is warranted. During what was arguably the first big cleantech boom around 2009, schemes were sold to angel investors thrilled to find a hot sector in the wake of the recent financial crash. Almost anything that sounded good got funded. Some of these schemes, such as a “clean”...

The post Commercializing Hydrocarbon Production from Air and Water first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] School districts should pay effective teachers what they’re worth, not dabble in affordable housing
item.description: USD 229 Blue Valley Gillian Chapman says teachers struggle to find affordable housing in the district, but instead of striking at the root of the self-inflicted problem, she formed a committee to explore housing options. She says the lack of stable, affordable housing is “absolutely an issue in Blue Valley, one that I actually did […]

item.title: [Grassrootinstitute] Curbside teller rules are obsolete, should be repealed
item.description:
July 15, 2026, 9 a.m. Honolulu Hale To: Honolulu City Council Committee on Infrastructure, Transportation & Technology Radiant Cordero, Chair Val A. Okimoto, Vice Chair From: Grassroot Institute of Hawaii Ted Kefalas, Director of Strategic Campaigns RE: Bill 35 (2026) — RELATING TO STREETS, SIDEWALKS, MALLS, AND OTHER PUBLIC PLACES Aloha Chair Cordero, Vice Chair […]

The post Curbside teller rules are obsolete, should be repealed appeared first on Grassroot Institute of Hawaii.

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] Massachusetts’s Financial Transparency: A Mixed Report Card
item.description:
Public reporting of state finances that is transparent, reliable, and timely is what the International Monetary Fund defines as fiscal transparency. Not only can the transparency of this information help governments respond appropriately to the ever-changing economy, but it can also help the public hold them accountable.  

The post Massachusetts’s Financial Transparency: A Mixed Report Card  appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] Options to eliminate property taxes
item.description: Calls to eliminate property taxes are echoing across the country, understandably generating tremendous enthusiasm among taxpayers and legislators. The desire is so intense that some Kansans are demanding the immediate elimination of property taxes. Eliminating property taxes altogether, or even on a single property classification such as residential, requires careful consideration of the cascading implications […]

item.title: [Cascadepolicy] Who’s Responsible for Oregon Wildfires?
item.description:
by Randal O’Toole Defensible Space Is Every Property Owner’s Responsibility Oregon has entered fire season earlier than usual this year, which means we are hearing repeated litanies from various interest groups. “If only we had more money to suppress fires,” says the Forest Service. “If only we were allowed to manage the forests” by cutting […]

The post Who’s Responsible for Oregon Wildfires? appeared first on Cascade Policy Institute.

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] Schug, a giant of economics education, took lessons beyond classrooms to anyone willing to learn
item.description:
Wisconsin is a better place because of Mark Schug’s scholarship, his advocacy, his teaching, and, above all, his kindness. His influence can be seen in the countless students, teachers, policymakers, and colleagues whose lives he touched.

The post Schug, a giant of economics education, took lessons beyond classrooms to anyone willing to learn appeared first on Badger Institute.

item.title: [Opportunityarkansas] Soros-backed group is bankrolling the campaign to rewrite Arkansas’s Constitution
item.description:
A George Soros-backed organization is helping fund the effort to amend the Arkansas Constitution, according to the latest campaign finance […]

The post Soros-backed group is bankrolling the campaign to rewrite Arkansas’s Constitution first appeared on Opportunity Arkansas.

item.title: [Idahofreedom] BREAKING: DHS Confirm Identity of Alleged Suspect in Boise Greenbelt Assault as Illegal Alien
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) confirmed to Idaho Freedom Foundation Wednesday that the suspect in the Boise Greenbelt assault and kidnapping case is an illegal alien from Mexico. “Javier Ortiz-Galvan is an alien from Mexico charged with aggravated assault and second-degree kidnapping. He entered the United States illegally at an unknown date and time […]

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] More Wisconsin circuit courts use text reminders to move justice along: a ‘godsend’
item.description:
More than 60 percent of Wisconsin circuit courts use text-message reminders to notify criminal defendants of upcoming court dates, but only a minority of counties send messages automatically.

The post More Wisconsin circuit courts use text reminders to move justice along: a ‘godsend’ appeared first on Badger Institute.

item.title: [Goldwaterinstitute] Liberty in Action: What 90% of Americans Agree On
item.description:
The Goldwater Institute has long fought for patients’ freedom to access lifesaving treatments without government bureaucrats standing in their way...

The post Liberty in Action: What 90% of Americans Agree On appeared first on Goldwater Institute.

item.title: [Mainepolicy] Portland’s 100-Foot Rule is One More Barrier to Doing Business
item.description:
By 2026 Maine Policy Institute Public Policy Intern Andrew Cook Portland’s downtown should be one of the easiest places in Maine to open a restaurant, bar, music venue, or other nightlife business. It is compact, walkable, and already attracts the customers these businesses need. At a time when Portland’s storefronts are sitting empty, the city should be […]

The post Portland’s 100-Foot Rule is One More Barrier to Doing Business appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Itrfoundation] Out of the Office and Into Conversations
item.description:
ITR Foundation's John Hendrickson was invited to speak in Urbandale on the historical influence of Reformed theology and the principles of limited government, constitutionalism, and liberty that helped shape the American founding. The presentation reflects an important part of our mission beyond research and media appearances: getting into communities across Iowa to engage directly with citizens, answer questions, and have thoughtful conversations about history, government, and public policy. Those conversations make our work stronger. Hearing directly from Iowans helps keep our research grounded in the concerns of everyday taxpayers, and we're always grateful for opportunities to bring these discussions to communities across the state.

The post Out of the Office and Into Conversations appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Jbartlett] Smaller, cheaper homes are coming to New Hampshire
item.description:
Last year, a bipartisan majority of legislators agreed to legalize manufactured homes in all residential zones in the state. But it never happened. Though House Bill 685 passed both chambers on voice votes, it died in conference committee.   The bill was an effort to open the housing market to a cheaper, smaller form of […]

The post Smaller, cheaper homes are coming to New Hampshire appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] Participatory budgeting won’t save California cities
item.description:
This spring, Marin County launched a second round of participatory budgeting, inviting residents to help decide how another $2.5 million in public funds should be spent. The program reflects California’s growing embrace of participatory budgeting as an innovative reform. The idea clearly resonates with many people. More participation should produce better government. It won’t. A...

The post Participatory budgeting won’t save California cities first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Goldwaterinstitute] Arizona Supreme Court Backs Goldwater in Phoenix Police Union Records Case
item.description:
In a victory for greater government transparency, the Arizona Supreme Court unanimously ruled in favor of the Goldwater Institute today..

The post Arizona Supreme Court Backs Goldwater in Phoenix Police Union Records Case appeared first on Goldwater Institute.

item.title: [Inpolicy] The Outstater
item.description: Women Journalists Stressed AN INDIANAPOLIS EDITOR is crusading for more gentlemanly treatment of female journalists. She says they are being merciless trolled by online critics, suspiciously male. After a generation of “I am woman; hear me roar,” we aren’t going to put that on our worry list. An editor for the Capital Chronicle cites a United Nations study Read the full article...

item.title: [Grassrootinstitute] Hawaii comes in first — at being the worst state for business
item.description:
For the first time in the 20 years that national news outlet CNBC has been ranking America’s top states for business, Hawaii came in dead last. In particular, the Aloha State finished 50th in terms of infrastructure and the cost-of-doing-business, and landed among the worst for its workforce, business friendliness, access to capital, cost of […]

The post Hawaii comes in first — at being the worst state for business appeared first on Grassroot Institute of Hawaii.

item.title: [Inpolicy] Keating: South Bend Utility Assistance
item.description: by Maryann O. Keating, Ph.D. For low-income households, putting food on the table is the highest priority-and rightly so. Yet electricity, water, heating and telephone bills strongly increase the stress levels of both low- and middle-income households. This month, approximately 41,500 households received a letter this from the South Bend Mayor describing the City’s Utility Read the full article...

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] Beyond Free College: Massachusetts’ Push to Make College More Affordable
item.description:
On June 26, Massachusetts Governor Maura Healey announced a three-year bachelor’s degree pilot program at Suffolk University and Merrimack College, aiming to cut tuition without sacrificing academic quality. As discussed in my previous blog, tuition costs have soared, and it may be due to the way universities fundamentally operate. In 2023, Massachusetts' average annual tuition neared $54,000 (Figure 1), with elite institutions costing even more.  

The post Beyond Free College: Massachusetts’ Push to Make College More Affordable  appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] Submit your comment against the 10 mile Chaco Canyon barrier
item.description:
The comment period is open from now until July 29, 2026. Submit your comment by clicking here today. Acting arbitrarily, the Biden Administration, led by Deb Haaland (who is running for Governor) adopted a 10 mile moratorium AROUND Chaco Canyon in 2023. The moratorium would prevent any development in the 336,400 acres surrounding the Chaco Culture National […]

The post Submit your comment against the 10 mile Chaco Canyon barrier appeared first on Rio Grande Foundation.

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] Idaho has a fiscal lesson to teach neighboring Washington
item.description: Idaho is being rewarded for its prudent budget policies. The Evergreen State is facing a business climate in distress from self-inflicted wounds. Unless Washington changes course, expect more of its citizens and businesses to head east to enjoy the benefits of the Gem State’s fiscal discipline.

item.title: [Jamesmadison] The Center Square: Artificial Intelligence a New Horizon for Florida’s Economy
item.description:
I grew up working Florida soil, the kind that teaches patience, persistence, and respect for what’s coming next...

The post The Center Square: Artificial Intelligence a New Horizon for Florida’s Economy appeared first on James Madison Institute.

item.title: [Yankeeinstitute] The Endorsement Is Done. The Next SEBAC Fight Has Already Started
item.description: Gov. Ned Lamont secured the Connecticut AFL-CIO’s endorsement last month after candidate interviews conducted behind closed doors. Now, one of the federation’s state employee affiliates is already organizing members around […]The post The Endorsement Is Done. The Next SEBAC Fight Has Already Started appeared first on Yankee Institute.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] Oregon Education Association Puts Politics Ahead of Students
item.description:
For years, families across the state have watched its education system struggle with low academic outcomes, classroom disruptions, staffing shortages and a growing sense that too many students are being pushed through schools that aren’t meeting their individual needs.

The post Oregon Education Association Puts Politics Ahead of Students appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Beacontn] Amicus Brief: Hershey v. City of Bossier City
item.description: When government officials violate the people’s fundamental constitutional rights, Americans deserve the ability to hold those officials accountable.

item.description:
Today, the Goldwater Institute filed suit challenging the validity of the signatures submitted for the destructive and misleadingly titled “Protect..

The post Goldwater Challenges Signatures of Anti-School Choice Measure appeared first on Goldwater Institute.

item.title: [Showmeinstitute] Declining Student Enrollment and a Growing Opportunity with Jude Schwalbach
item.description:
Susan Pendergrass speaks with Jude Schwalbach, senior policy analyst at Reason Foundation, about declining K-12 enrollment nationwide and what it means for the future of open enrollment. They discuss why […]

The post Declining Student Enrollment and a Growing Opportunity with Jude Schwalbach appeared first on Show-Me Institute.

item.title: [Mackinac] Unlock opportunity to make Michigan more affordable
item.description: Senate bills 1055, 1056 and 1057 would make it easier to find work

item.title: [Sutherlandinstitute] How the federal government keeps expanding its power | Kevin Williamson & Bill Duncan
item.description:
They discuss regulatory “dark matter,” the decline of congressional responsibility, practical examples of federal agencies pressuring states, and how federalism, transparency, and a renewed commitment to separation of powers could help rein in Washington’s influence.

The post How the federal government keeps expanding its power | Kevin Williamson & Bill Duncan appeared first on Sutherland Institute.

item.title: [Yankeeinstitute] Beyond the Classroom: What Connecticut’s Teachers Union Focused on in Denver
item.description: When Connecticut Education Association (CEA) delegates traveled to Denver for the National Education Association’s (NEA) July 3–7 Representative Assembly, they weren’t simply attending another annual convention. They were participating in […]The post Beyond the Classroom: What Connecticut’s Teachers Union Focused on in Denver  appeared first on Yankee Institute.

item.title: [Americanexperiment] More transparency is needed about the 1 in 4 chronically absent Minnesota students
item.description:
Today, the majority of Minnesota students cannot read or do math at grade level. One strong contributor is the fact that many students don’t attend class. In Minnesota, there’s been a significant decline in actual school attendance. In 2024, about one in four Minnesota students were chronically absent. Unfortunately, Minnesota’s school system is not designed...

The post More transparency is needed about the 1 in 4 chronically absent Minnesota students appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Empowerms] Sen. Jeremy England: Neighbors Before Opponents
item.description:
Sen. Jeremy England: Neighbors Before Opponents Politics works best when we remember we’re neighbors before we’re opponents. But in an age of polarization, is that still possible? In this episode, Grant sits down with State Senator Jeremy England to discuss the experiences and convictions that have shaped his approach to public service. From growing up…

The post Sen. Jeremy England: Neighbors Before Opponents first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Texaspolicy] The Staggering Amount of New Debt Added in May 2026
item.description:
On May 2nd, 2026, Texas voters did something astonishing. They approved more new debt in that single afternoon than the entire gross domestic product of the country of Uganda.    The $76.4 billion in new principal debt authorized that day won’t be issued tomorrow; but with voter approval in-hand, local governments now have the go-ahead to borrow more and add onto the $368.3 billion in principal debt already owed. Of course, these totals...

The post The Staggering Amount of New Debt Added in May 2026 first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Alabamapolicy] Alabama Policy Institute Awarded 2026 Ed-Prize for Innovative Opportunity Anchors Initiative
item.description:
BIRMINGHAM, Ala. – July 20, 2026 – The Alabama Policy Institute (API) is proud to announce that API has been named a recipient of the 2026 Ed-Prize by the State Policy Network (SPN). This prestigious award recognizes API’s commitment to bold, scalable innovation in K–12 education through its Opportunity Anchors Initiative. The Ed-Prize is awarded to organizations […]

The post Alabama Policy Institute Awarded 2026 Ed-Prize for Innovative Opportunity Anchors Initiative appeared first on Alabama Policy Institute.

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] Special Delivery: Drone Technology in Our State
item.description:
Louisiana is positioning itself as a leader in welcoming new and emerging technologies. From Waymos learning the streets of New Orleans, the space satellites delivering high speed internet to rural communities, the LSU football players receiving care from AI-powered stethoscopes, and now, the drones carrying packages to Baton Rouge residents.  Last week, Baton Rouge became...

The post Special Delivery: Drone Technology in Our State appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.title: [Idahofreedom] Spy Cameras Are Flocking To Idaho – Your City Could Be Next
item.description: Automated License Plate Readers (ALPRs) are sweeping across Idaho and prompting fears that the Gem State may soon become another surveillance state. Some residents, however, are pushing back against police departments using a private company to collect data on Idahoans’ vehicles.   Flock cameras, a technology from the Georgia-based Flock Safety company, are ostensibly used to […]

item.title: [Showmeinstitute] Missouri Earns a “B” on Cell-Phones-in-Schools Report Card
item.description:
Listen to this article Missouri received a “B” on a national report card evaluating state policies on cell phone use in schools. This grade, available at phonefreeschoolsreport.org, places Missouri in […]

The post Missouri Earns a “B” on Cell-Phones-in-Schools Report Card appeared first on Show-Me Institute.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] Kansas School Board Resource Center Welcomes Marc Woofter
item.description: The Kansas School Board Resource Center (KSBRC) is pleased to announce that veteran Kansas educator and school leader Marc Woofter has joined the organization as Project Lead. With over three decades of experience in public education, Woofter brings extensive expertise in school leadership, district administration, board governance, curriculum development, and student achievement initiatives. In his […]

item.title: [Americanexperiment] Minneapolis CHAOS: Crime, Violence, and Leaders with NO ANSWERS
item.description:
Our “leaders” have no answers after ANOTHER violent weekend in Minneapolis Check out the American Experiment Podcast!   Catch up on the violence that plagued Minneapolis over the weekend, the latest numbers documenting Minnesota’s education crisis, and an insane story about a man who committed a fraud SO brazen it will make you wonder “why...

The post Minneapolis CHAOS: Crime, Violence, and Leaders with NO ANSWERS appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Americanexperiment] US border security and immigration enforcement – undeniable success amidst the noise
item.description:
There has been a concerted effort by “open immigration” advocates, “anti-Trumpers,” and a supportive legacy media, to portray the immigration enforcement efforts of the Trump administration in the poorest light possible. Many detractors have virtually ignored the success the Trump administration has had at securing the border, while others have suggested the administration has not...

The post US border security and immigration enforcement – undeniable success amidst the noise appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] Louisiana’s Test Scores Are Rising. So are the Number of Children Still Waiting for a School that Fits.
item.description:
Louisiana’s public schools have become a national talking point. Two straight rounds of gains on the National Assessment of Educational Progress (NAEP), plus a record-high showing on the state’s own LEAP exam released this month, have officials calling the results historic. They’re not wrong. But look at what the Legislature did in the same breath:...

The post Louisiana’s Test Scores Are Rising. So are the Number of Children Still Waiting for a School that Fits. appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.title: [Sutherlandinstitute] Important questions answered about the federal scholarship tax credit
item.description:
States must opt into the program for families to receive scholarships. Utah has already opted in. Regardless of what a state decides, individuals from any state can donate to any approved SGOs in another participating state and still receive a tax credit.

The post Important questions answered about the federal scholarship tax credit appeared first on Sutherland Institute.

item.title: [Mackinac] How does MEDC chief’s defense of business subsidies hold up?
item.description: A line-by-line look at Quentin Messer’s case for embattled development agency

item.title: [Pacificresearch] California’s Missing Report: Why the State Needs an Annual State of the Criminal Justice System Report
item.description:
Every year, state agencies, universities, advocacy organizations, and think tanks—including the Pacific Research Institute—publish valuable analyses of crime, courts, corrections, sentencing, policing, and public safety. Those reports shape legislation, influence budgets, and inform public debate. Yet despite this steady stream of research, California continues to revisit many of the same policy debates year after year […]

The post California’s Missing Report: Why the State Needs an Annual State of the Criminal Justice System Report appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Illinoispolicy] Drivers have less than 2 weeks to weigh in on endless toll hikes, years of congestion
item.description:
Illinois drivers have only a short window to comment on a $26.5 billion capital plan that includes the largest passenger tollway hike in state history.

The post Drivers have less than 2 weeks to weigh in on endless toll hikes, years of congestion appeared first on Illinois Policy.

item.title: [Alabamapolicy] Alabama Policy Institute earns national Ed-Prize for school choice initiative- Yellowhammer News
item.description:
The Alabama Policy Institute has been awarded the 2026 Ed-Prize by the State Policy Network, a national organization of state-based public policy groups, in recognition of its Opportunity Anchors Initiative, an effort designed to expand educational options for Alabama families ahead of universal CHOOSE Act eligibility in the 2027-28 school year. The Ed-Prize recognizes organizations developing innovative […]

The post Alabama Policy Institute earns national Ed-Prize for school choice initiative- Yellowhammer News appeared first on Alabama Policy Institute.

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] No surprise! West Coast climate policies drive the high cost of gas
item.description: Washington’s ongoing denial of the impact its climate policies are having on the price of gas is in stark contrast to California, with similar policies. The Golden State at least has the honesty to acknowledge the cost increases for drivers.

item.title: [Sutherlandinstitute] What political hot takes miss about the Supreme Court
item.description:
It deeply distorts what is actually happening. Some of the decisions of the just-concluded court session make this clear.

The post What political hot takes miss about the Supreme Court appeared first on Sutherland Institute.

item.title: [Itrfoundation] Trade and Tariffs Take Center Stage at Summit
item.description:
Iowa agriculture remains under financial pressure, with farm incomes down 53 percent from 2022 to 2024 amid low commodity prices, rising costs, and trade uncertainty. The Iowa Farm Bureau's 2026 Economic Summit featured competing views on trade, with Samuel Gregg criticizing tariffs while former U.S. Trade Representative Robert Lighthizer defended them as necessary to achieve balanced trade. The discussion highlighted the stakes for Iowa, where both agriculture and manufacturing depend heavily on international markets and future trade policy decisions.

The post Trade and Tariffs Take Center Stage at Summit appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Pacificresearch] Approach online weight-loss drug offers with skepticism
item.description:
If you’ve spent any time on social media lately, you’ve probably seen the ads. Lose weight fast. Get access to GLP-1s. Skip the doctor’s office. Delivered right to your door. For millions of Americans looking to lose weight, the promise is understandably appealing. These remarkable drugs, which include Ozempic, Wegovy, Mounjaro and Zepbound, have become […]

The post Approach online weight-loss drug offers with skepticism appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Texaspolicy] Restoring Confidence and Prestige to the Teaching Profession
item.description:
Restoring Confidence and Prestige to the Teaching Profession The epidemic of uncertified teachers in Texas schools is the result of the collapse of the teacher preparation program, especially university programs. These programs have traded a focus on the classroom for social activism. Key points: More than 10% of teachers in Texas schools are now uncertified....

The post Restoring Confidence and Prestige to the Teaching Profession first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Texaspolicy] Ineffective University Educator Preparation is the Root of the Teaching Profession’s Woes
item.description:
For the last two legislative sessions, certification and attrition have dominated the teacher pipeline discussion, but if legislators wish to ensure a sufficient supply of effective teachers in Texas classrooms, they must reform the root of the problem, university schools of education. To say that the teaching profession has been in upheaval for the better...

The post Ineffective University Educator Preparation is the Root of the Teaching Profession’s Woes first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Empirecenter] Empire Center Poll: New Yorkers Prioritize Low Energy Prices and Have Little Appetite for Radical Climate Policies
item.description:
FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE ALBANY, N.Y. — New Yorkers across political, geographic, age, and income groups oppose energy and climate policies....

The post Empire Center Poll: New Yorkers Prioritize Low Energy Prices and Have Little Appetite for Radical Climate Policies appeared first on Empire Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Empirecenter] Opinion Poll – Energy
item.description:
Executive Summary New York voters prioritize affordable energy and show limited support for energy and climate policies that could increase....

The post Opinion Poll – Energy appeared first on Empire Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Pacificresearch] Californians have a rare chance to rein in CEQA and support housing
item.description:
Curious who‘s to blame for California’s housing crisis? Just look at who wants to kill the cure. A measure to reform the chief impediment to building homes in the state just qualified for the November ballot, and the same week, labor unions and environmental groups dumped millions into the effort to defeat it. Lawmakers in […]

The post Californians have a rare chance to rein in CEQA and support housing appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Inpolicy] Eichenberger: The Entitlement Trap
item.description: by Dan Eichenberger M.D. In 2025, the U.S. federal government is projected to spend around $7 trillion — roughly 23 percent of the nation’s entire economic output. More than half of that spending goes to mandatory “entitlement” programs such as Social Security, Medicare, Medicaid, and other benefits. Social Security alone accounts for about 22 percent of Read the full article...

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] Every Cause Has Its Champion—and Maybe a Budget Carve-Out In Louisiana
item.description:
The Pelican Institute has repeatedly highlighted the drawbacks of Louisiana’s hundreds of statutorily dedicated funds and fund accounts—carved-out pots of money reserved for specific purposes that reduce lawmakers’ ability to set spending priorities each year. Today, let’s take a look at some of the stranger ones still on the books. The Sweet Potato Pests and...

The post Every Cause Has Its Champion—and Maybe a Budget Carve-Out In Louisiana appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.title: [Itrfoundation] This November, Iowa Taxpayers Are On The Ballot
item.description:
On Tuesday, November 3, 2026, Iowa voters will have the opportunity to place a permanent taxpayer protection into the state constitution.

The proposed amendment, which would make it harder for politicians to raise your taxes, has already been approved twice by the Iowa Legislature. Now, the question will be decided by Iowa voters at the ballot box.

The post This November, Iowa Taxpayers Are On The Ballot appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Rifreedom] New Report Shows RI’s Big Gov’t Gamble has Failed Residents
item.description:
Rhode Island’s Liberal Policies are Driving Residents and Jobs to Conservative states. Ocean Staters have suffered significant personal income loss compared to Red states For decades, Rhode Island’s political class […]

The post New Report Shows RI’s Big Gov’t Gamble has Failed Residents appeared first on RI Center for Freedom and Prosperity.

item.title: [Yankeeinstitute] Y It Matters: Why Informed Citizens Sustain Self-Government
item.description: Earlier this month, like most Americans, my family and I celebrated the 250th birthday of our nation. It’s remarkable to realize that the United States is the first nation founded […]The post Y It Matters: Why Informed Citizens Sustain Self-Government appeared first on Yankee Institute.

item.title: [Showmeinstitute] Missouri Doubles Down on Failed Economic Development Scheme
item.description:
Gov. Mike Kehoe recently signed House Bill (HB) 3231, a broad economic development package that revives the Missouri Downtown Economic Stimulus Act, better known as MODESA. According to the Kansas […]

The post Missouri Doubles Down on Failed Economic Development Scheme appeared first on Show-Me Institute.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] You can’t legislate member loyalty
item.description:
Oregon politicians can restrict outreach. They can threaten lawsuits, protect government unions and make it harder for public employees to receive information about their constitutional rights.

The post You can’t legislate member loyalty appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Grassrootinstitute] All county bills should come with fiscal impact statements
item.description:
July 22, 2026, 10 a.m. Hawai`i County Building To: Hawaii County Council Committee on Finance Matt Kaneali`I-Kleinfelder, Chair James E. Hustace, Vice Chair From: Grassroot Institute of Hawaii Jonathan Helton, Policy Analyst Re: Bill 171 (2026) — RELATING TO FISCAL IMPACT STATEMENTS Aloha Chair Kaneali`I-Kleinfelder, Vice Chair Hustace and other members of the Committee, The […]

The post All county bills should come with fiscal impact statements appeared first on Grassroot Institute of Hawaii.

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] Study: Tax Cut Would Have Grown State Economy, Increased Wages and Reduced Unemployment
item.description:
One percentage point reduction in state income tax would have also reduced outmigration from Massachusetts BOSTON – A proposal to cut the state income tax rate from 5 percent to 4 percent over three years would have provided the median Massachusetts household...

The post Study: Tax Cut Would Have Grown State Economy, Increased Wages and Reduced Unemployment appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] MSPC awarded National Civics Prize
item.description: The national award recognizes MSPC's "We the Students" Civics Bowl, a fast-paced, buzzer-based competition that moves civic education beyond memorization and into active learning. The program challenges high school students on American government, constitutional principles, free enterprise, and individual rights while encouraging teamwork, critical thinking, and civic engagement.

item.title: [Johnlocke] Journalism group hides mistake involving terrorists
item.description:
Adam Kredo writes for the Washington Free Beacon about a major journalism group covering its tracks after making a blunder. The International Federation of Journalists (IFJ), the world’s largest advocacy group for reporters, quietly removed, with no public acknowledgment, eight confirmed terrorists from its widely cited list of journalists killed in Gaza. The expurgations, revealed...

The post Journalism group hides mistake involving terrorists appeared first on John Locke Foundation.

item.title: [Johnlocke] California mandate creates lucrative business for new NGO
item.description:
Chris Bray writes for the Federalist about a key beneficiary of a new California regulation. [C]ompanies that make or distribute any form of textiles in California are required to start making payments this year to a nongovernmental organization (NGO) that has been designated by the state as the official “producer responsibility organization” (PRO), which will...

The post California mandate creates lucrative business for new NGO appeared first on John Locke Foundation.

item.title: [Johnlocke] NJ governor admits hundreds of foreigners voted
item.description:
Kevin Killough writes for Just the News about a disturbing development for election integrity. A blue-state governor has admitted a fact that most Democrats refuse to believe. New Jersey Gov. Mikie Sherrill is blaming a software glitch in the state’s motor vehicle system for accidentally registering 6,660 noncitizens to vote.  She further acknowledged Tuesday as...

The post NJ governor admits hundreds of foreigners voted appeared first on John Locke Foundation.

item.description:
IFT represents educators in more than 200 districts across Illinois. But teachers’ zip codes dictate how much support they actually get from the union.

item.title: [Illinoispolicy] Rockford close to zoning reforms to increase housing
item.description:
The proposed amendments would allow greater housing density, eliminate garage mandates and reduce approval hurdles for new developments.
